articles.title: State mulls to replace palm oil with mustard oil for PDS
articles.description: In an innovative move, Jharkhand's food department is considering a shift from palm oil to mustard oil within the public distribution system, aiming to enhance the nutrition of 2.69 crore beneficiaries. Minister Irfan Ansari has championed the health advantages of mustard oil, emphasizing its potential to benefit families statewide.

articles.title: Union Cabinet raises sugarcane FRP by ₹10 per quintal
articles.description: Union Cabinet raises sugarcane FRP by ₹10 to support farmers, ensuring fair prices and no deductions for low recovery rates.

articles.title: Capricorn Horoscope Today, May 6, 2026: If you keep spending to comfort yourself, the stress will double tomorrow
articles.description: As we navigate through today, it's crucial to tread carefully amid financial pressures and family tensions. Steer clear of rash purchases and avoid contentious conversations, particularly with your siblings. In your relationships, strive for peaceful dialogue instead of disputes to maintain a serene home environment. Professionally, temper your aspirations and concentrate on what is feasible.

articles.title: Delay in utilisation certificate submission hits varsity finances
articles.description: Bihar's state universities are grappling with a severe financial crisis as they struggle with delayed salary payments for staff. This issue arises from their ongoing inability to present utilisation certificates for government grants, leaving over Rs 600 crore in funds unutilized.

articles.title: BT Explainer: Term Insurance — When to buy, ideal cover, key factors simplified
articles.description: Financial planners consistently recommend buying a term plan as early as possible, ideally when an individual starts earning. The logic is simple: premiums are significantly lower at a younger age and remain fixed throughout the policy tenure.

articles.title: Gold Silver Rate Today (May 5): Metals Climb As Safe Haven, Check Current Rates Across Major Cities In India
articles.description:
Gold Silver Rate Today: Gold and silver prices moved higher on Tuesday, supported by fresh buying interest and firm global cues, with both precious metals recording gains in futures trade.

On the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX), gold futures for August delivery advanced by Rs 254, or 0.17 per cent, to trade at Rs 1,53,100 per 10 grams, with a turnover of 74 lots. Analysts attributed the rise to fresh positions created by market participants, backed by steady demand in the spot market.

Tracking the domestic trend, international gold prices also strengthened. In New York, gold futures were trading 0.50 per cent higher at $4,544.62 per ounce.

Silver prices also edged up on Tuesday, supported by geopolitical concerns, particularly tensions in West Asia, which kept safe-haven demand intact.

On the MCX, silver futures for July delivery rose by Rs 225 to Rs 2,44,120 per kg in a turnover of 982 lots. This marked an increase of 0.09 per cent in futures trade. Analysts said the uptick was driven by fresh buying activity among traders.

In the global market, Comex silver futures climbed 2.36 per cent to $78.29 per ounce, reflecting continued strength in international prices.

articles.title: Fear vs facts: Why nuclear power's revival is crucial for a cleaner, secure energy future
articles.description: Despite past fears from Chernobyl and Fukushima, nuclear energy is re-emerging as a reliable, clean power source. France's reliance showcases low emissions, while Germany's shutdown increased fossil fuel use. Modern reactors boast enhanced safety features, and global expansion is underway with new designs like SMRs promising efficiency and a smaller environmental footprint.

articles.title: Market Strategy Now: Where Should You Invest? | Shweta Rajani Explains
articles.description: The market is stuck in a tight range, but opportunities are quietly building beneath the surface. As the Nifty hovers between 23,900 and 24,400, the real action is shifting to the broader markets. Midcaps and smallcaps are clearly outperforming, backed by strong Q4 earnings momentum. Shweta Rajani of Anand Rathi Wealth highlights that while large caps delivered a muted ~6% growth, mid and small caps are clocking an impressive 30–40% profit growth so far. Banking and financials remain steady contributors, while sectoral trends are becoming more stock-specific. The key strategy now is balance - allocating smartly across market caps and staying invested for the long term. With valuations in a fair zone and earnings visibility improving, disciplined investing - not timing the market - remains the winning approach.

articles.title: Marico Q4 net profit rises 14% to Rs 391 crore
articles.description: Consolidated reveue from operations rose 22 per cent to Rs 3,333 crore in Q4, up from Rs 2,730 crore, reported last year. However, on a sequential basis, revenue fell from Rs 3,537 crore reported in Q3, the company said in a regulatory filing on May 5.

articles.title: Markets After Election Results: BJP Win, Political Stability | Where Should You Invest Now?
articles.description: Markets are navigating a volatile phase as global cues and domestic triggers collide. Despite a soft start and the Nifty slipping below 24,000, stability signals from recent state election results have caught the attention of global brokerages. Firms like Jefferies, Macquarie, and Citi highlight stronger political continuity, which could aid long-term policy execution. However, concerns around rising populism, fiscal discipline, and shifting state spending patterns remain key watchpoints. Earnings season is driving stock-specific action, with names like BHEL, Tata Technologies, and Wockhardt reacting sharply to results and brokerage views. Experts suggest limited long-term market impact from elections, while emphasizing consumption trends and earnings resilience. Sectorally, metals and IT show strength, while broader markets remain highly selective. Investors are advised to stay cautious yet focused on fundamentals.

articles.description: Protect your home this summer by following these six essential AC safety steps. articles.title: NTPC peer IPO coming soon? BJP win in Bengal raises hopes of Damodar Valley listing
articles.description: Damodar Valley Corporation's stock market debut is now more likely. The Bharatiya Janata Party's win in West Bengal has removed a key political obstacle. This development is expected to speed up the initial public offering process. DVC operates in power generation, transmission, and distribution. The listing will offer investors a new opportunity in the eastern power sector.

articles.title: Sobha rises after Q4 PAT spurts 125% YoY to Rs 92 cr
articles.description: Sobha rose 3.29% to Rs 1,485.60 after the company reported a 124.8% surge in consolidated net profit to Rs 91.84 crore on a 60.2% rise in revenue from operations to Rs 1,987.84 crore in Q4 FY26 over Q4 FY25.

articles.title: Larsen & Toubro bags coal-to-ammonia nitrate project in Odisha
articles.description: L&T Energy Hydrocarbon Onshore has won a large order from Bharat Coal Gasification and Chemicals (BCGCL), a joint venture of Coal India (CIL) and Bharat Heavy Electricals (BHEL), for a Coal-to-Ammonia-Nitrate project in Odisha. According to the company's project classification, the order is valued between Rs 2,500 crore to Rs 5,000 crore.

articles.title: JioBlackRock Mutual Fund files draft document with Sebi for hybrid long-short fund under Prism SIF
articles.description: JioBlackRock Mutual Fund has filed draft papers with Sebi to launch a hybrid long-short fund under its Prism SIF platform. The interval strategy will invest across equity, debt and derivatives with limited short exposure. With structured allocation and income strategies, the fund targets long-term capital appreciation alongside income generation for investors.

articles.title: OpenAI’s nonprofit-to-profit shift faces scrutiny as president Brockman reveals $30 billion stake
articles.description: OpenAI president Greg Brockman disclosed his nearly $30 billion stake during Elon Musk’s lawsuit over the AI company’s nonprofit origins and shift toward profit.

articles.title: Why LNG supply disruption due to West Asia war has pushed India to consider raising storage capacity
articles.description: India depends on LNG imports to meet about half of its natural gas requirement, and about 60% of those imports came through the critical maritime chokepoint of the Strait of Hormuz, primarily from Qatar and the UAE.

articles.title: Vertex Group acquires $4M digital CX business
articles.description: This move is part of Vertex Group’s robust growth agenda, which includes targeting more than two strategic acquisitions annually over the next three years.

articles.title: Jindal Stainless sees 7–9% growth, flags West Asia crisis impact on demand
articles.description: Jindal Stainless anticipates a volume growth of seven to nine percent this fiscal year. The company is preparing for potential impacts from the West Asia crisis on demand. They plan to invest ₹2,600 crore in capital expenditure. Jindal Stainless will review its growth target mid-fiscal. Exports are expected to contribute about ten percent of sales.

articles.title: Can you get another loan if you’re already paying EMIs? Here’s the answer
articles.description: Your salary is just the start for loan approval. Lenders scrutinize your income after deducting all current EMIs. This remaining amount, measured by the Fixed Obligation to Income Ratio, dictates your borrowing capacity. Keeping this ratio below 50% is key. Existing EMIs significantly reduce how much more you can borrow. Even small EMI reductions can boost your loan eligibility.

articles.title: Adani Cement eyes measured growth, defers ambitious FY28 targets
articles.description: Adani Cement is prioritizing increased utilization of its current facilities. The company will focus on optimizing existing capacities. Capital expenditure plans may be adjusted. The CEO indicated a potential shift in targets to FY30. This strategy aims to leverage existing assets effectively. Adani Cement currently has a production capacity of 109 million tonnes.

articles.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq Fall From Record High With Mideast Tensions in Focus
articles.description: Only energy gained as oil climbed after the US and Iran exchanged fire in the Persian Gulf.

articles.title: Oil near $114 as US-Iran fire jolts ceasefire and puts inflation scare back on Wall Street
articles.description: Oil holds near $114 as US-Iran clashes around Strait of Hormuz revive inflation fears and rattle global markets.

articles.title: Sebi seeks to align securitisation framework with RBI regulations
articles.description: Sebi has proposed easing securitisation norms, including relaxing the 25% single borrower exposure cap, to align with RBI regulations for entities governed by central bank rules. The regulator also suggested shifting disclosure responsibilities to the servicer and modifying governance for SPDEs, aiming to facilitate single-asset deals and enhance transparency.

articles.title: IndusInd's ex-CFO files Rs 70-cr suit over wrongful termination
articles.description: Former IndusInd Bank CFO Gobind Jain has filed a ₹70-crore lawsuit against the bank in the Bombay High Court, alleging wrongful termination. Jain seeks ₹20 crore for lost earnings and ₹50 crore for reputational damage and mental trauma. The suit details Jain's repeated attempts to resign and his calls for an external audit into alleged accounting lapses.
